analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 9 finding(s) from 9 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: jtag_unlock is generated by dmi_jtag but the visible top-level DMI request path into dm_top is not gated by jtag_unlock; SimDTM can drive the same DMI path without visible authorization.
findings.vulnerability_category: issue DMI/debug requests that can drive debug module state, debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, and dmactive_o / dm_top debug module DMI interface and outputs ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, debug_req_o / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external JTAG/DTM and simulation DTM interfaces Operation: issue DMI/debug requests that can drive debug module state, debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, and dmactive_o Object: dm_top debug module DMI interface and outputs ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, debug_req_o Expected guard: jtag_unlock or equivalent debug authorization should gate DMI request validity/contents before reaching dm_top and should cover simulation debug paths or make them unavailable Observed behavior: dmi_jtag outputs jtag_unlock, debug_req, and debug_req_valid. The available evidence states jtag_unlock is passed to reglk_wrapper, but not used in the visible debug_req_valid/debug_req path into dm_top. Under RISCV_FESVR_SIM, SimDTM directly drives debug_req_valid and debug_req fields without a jtag_unlock connection. Path: tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i -> dmi_jtag -> debug_req/debug_req_valid -> dm_top.dmi_req_i/dmi_req_valid_i -> dm_top debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o; RISCV_FESVR_SIM SimDTM -> same debug_req/debug_req_valid -> dm_top Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Unauthenticated JTAG/DTM or simulation DTM traffic may issue DMI operations that cause hart debug entry, non-debug-module reset, dmactive changes, or debug-module register access.

findings.summary: Access-control coverage is inconsistent: many wrappers receive acc_ctrl_c and boot ROM gates rom_req, but CLINT, PLIC, and debug NoC paths are visibly connected without acc_ctrl mediation.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write/configure/trigger through NoC-to-AXI-lite peripheral interfaces / boot ROM, crypto/wrapper peripherals, reset controls, reglk/acct control registers, DMA wrapper, CLINT/PLIC/debug-module access paths, fuse path / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software-visible NoC/AXI requesters at riscv_peripherals Operation: read/write/configure/trigger through NoC-to-AXI-lite peripheral interfaces Object: boot ROM, crypto/wrapper peripherals, reset controls, reglk/acct control registers, DMA wrapper, CLINT/PLIC/debug-module access paths, fuse path Expected guard: all security-sensitive peripheral requests should be mediated by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] and applicable lock bits Observed behavior: acc_ctrl_c is generated from acc_ctrl and indexed by priv_lvl_i. Boot ROM explicitly gates rom_req with acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]. Multiple peripheral wrappers receive acct_ctrl_i(acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][index]) and reglk_ctrl slices. However CLINT and debug NoC bridges connect directly to clint_axi_req and dm_master; PLIC bridge/logic is shown without acc_ctrl gating in the available evidence. Path: NoC inputs -> noc_axilite_bridge instances -> peripheral AXI requests; bootrom has rom_req_acct && acc_ctrl_c guard and wrappers receive acct_ctrl_i, while CLINT/PLIC/debug bridges connect directly to target request channels Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Privilege/accounting policy may not cover CLINT interrupt-control state, PLIC configuration, and debug NoC access; unauthorized NoC requesters may retain direct MMIO reachability to those targets.

findings.summary: DMA-visible lock behavior is not sticky or consistently enforced: dma_wrapper receives reglk_ctrl_i but write logic is gated only by en && we; core_lock_reg can be cleared and is reset by rst_8.
findings.vulnerability_category: write, clear, reset, and use lock/access-control state / reglk_ctrl, acc_ctrl, rst_1..rst_13, DMA core_lock_reg and DMA configuration registers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software-visible register interface and test/control inputs Operation: write, clear, reset, and use lock/access-control state Object: reglk_ctrl, acc_ctrl, rst_1..rst_13, DMA core_lock_reg and DMA configuration registers Expected guard: lock/control registers should be authorized, sticky/non-clearable when intended, and enforced on downstream configuration writes; resets should not reopen unauthorized configuration windows Observed behavior: Top-level passes reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl to many wrappers. In dma_wrapper, acct_ctrl_i only gates en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i. The observed write block uses if(en && we) with no reglk_ctrl_i check. core_lock_reg update allows a nonzero core_lock_reg to be cleared by a subsequent nonzero write, and reset condition including rst_8 clears all DMA registers including core_lock_reg. Path: NoC DMA request -> dma_axilite_bridge -> dma_wrapper en/we/address/wdata -> core_lock_reg/start_reg/length_reg/source/dest registers; reglk_ctrl[8] is connected to dma_wrapper but not observed in write gating Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Software-visible locks can be cleared or ignored for DMA configuration, enabling reconfiguration after a supposed lock and potential unauthorized DMA transfers.

findings.summary: DMA transfer progression uses PMP checks, but the observed pmp_allow_reg update overwrites rather than accumulates allow state, so only the most recent checked address may determine progression; start/config writes are separately only acct-gated at wrapper level.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure and trigger DMA read/write transfer / memory regions addressed by DMA source_addr_* and dest_addr_*, DMA start/valid controls / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI software requester configuring DMA Operation: configure and trigger DMA read/write transfer Object: memory regions addressed by DMA source_addr_* and dest_addr_*, DMA start/valid controls Expected guard: DMA start/valid generation must be gated by PMP allow/range checks for source and destination and by access-control/lock authorization for configuration writes Observed behavior: dma saves start/length/source/dest inputs in idle/done, then uses an FSM to check load and store ranges with pmp_addr_new and pmp_access_type_new. In the observed store check, pmp_allow_new is assigned pmp_data_allow instead of pmp_allow_reg && pmp_data_allow. The transition to CTRL_STORE uses (pmp_allow_reg && pmp_data_allow), but because pmp_allow_reg is overwritten during iterations, earlier denial can be lost if a later pmp_data_allow is true. Similar load path details are partially truncated, so complete source/dest coverage is uncertain. valid_new is asserted in CTRL_LOAD/CTRL_STORE/CTRL_DONE once the FSM reaches those states. Path: AXI-lite interface -> dma_wrapper start/length/source/dest registers -> dma core start_i/source/dest/length -> pmp_addr_reg/pmp_access_type_reg -> pmp allow -> valid_o/valid_reg transfer indicators Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A DMA transfer may proceed after one denied address in a multi-beat range if later checked addresses are allowed, potentially permitting access outside PMP-authorized memory.

findings.summary: dma_wrapper register writes are acct_ctrl gated but not visibly gated by reglk_ctrl_i or core_lock_reg; we_flag is connected to dma/dma_wrapper but its exact role is not resolved from the visible write-enable logic.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure DMA registers / DMA control/start, length, source_addr_lsb/msb, dest_addr_lsb/msb, core_lock_reg, done/end registers / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI software requester or external we_flag/test/control input Operation: write/configure DMA registers Object: DMA control/start, length, source_addr_lsb/msb, dest_addr_lsb/msb, core_lock_reg, done/end registers Expected guard: writes to DMA control/address registers should be blocked when lock bits or accounting/access-control disable the subject; external we_flag/test inputs must not bypass the lock Observed behavior: dma_wrapper instantiates axi_lite_interface and gates en with acct_ctrl_i. The observed write side resets registers on reset/rst_8 and, otherwise, writes registers when en && we. No reglk_ctrl_i or core_lock_reg check is present in the observed write block. core_lock_reg is writable and clearable as described in the lock-sticky record. we_flag is a wrapper and dma core input, but available write-block evidence does not show it as a proper lock guard. Path: dma_wrapper AXI register writes -> axi_lite_interface address/en_acct/we/wdata -> en=en_acct&&acct_ctrl_i -> write case for core_lock/start/length/source/dest/done/end -> dma core inputs Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An authorized-at-accounting requester can modify DMA configuration after lock bits are asserted, and any external/test we_flag may represent an unresolved alternate control path.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The precise use of we_flag in the full dma_wrapper/dma core was not fully visible; candidate is based on absence of lock guards in observed write path.

findings.summary: CLINT NoC-to-AXI path has no visible acc_ctrl/privilege mediation before reaching CLINT, and axi_lite_interface itself has no privilege input or denial/error response mechanism.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write CLINT MMIO registers / CLINT timer/mtimecmp/software interrupt control registers and timer_irq_o/ipi_o outputs / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI/NoC requester accessing CLINT MMIO Operation: read/write CLINT MMIO registers Object: CLINT timer/mtimecmp/software interrupt control registers and timer_irq_o/ipi_o outputs Expected guard: CLINT register accesses should preserve requester/privilege mediation from the surrounding access-control layer; no AXI-lite handshake should generate en/we for unauthorized subjects or out-of-range addresses Observed behavior: The top-level CLINT bridge connects NoC signals directly to clint_axi_req/clint_axi_resp. No acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl/priv_lvl_i gate is shown for the CLINT instance or bridge. axi_lite_interface accepts AXI requests and produces en_o/we_o/address_o without privilege/accounting inputs; it returns OKAY responses by tying response fields to zero. Path: buf_ariane_clint_noc2_* -> i_clint_axilite_bridge -> clint_axi_req.aw/ar/w -> clint instance -> timer_irq_o/ipi_o; standalone axi_lite_interface path: ariane_axi -> address_o/en_o/we_o/data_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Unauthorized NoC requesters may write timer/software interrupt state and influence timer_irq_o/ipi_o because the visible path does not mediate by privilege/access-control.

findings.summary: Fuse access is routed through pkt_wrapper with acct_ctrl_i and reglk_ctrl_i, but internal fuse read authorization and denied-read behavior are not visible; no direct unguarded top-level fuse response mux was proven.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/expose fuse data / fuse_rdata and fuse-addressed protected configuration/secret values / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software-visible bus/NoC requester or debug/test requester Operation: read/expose fuse data Object: fuse_rdata and fuse-addressed protected configuration/secret values Expected guard: fuse reads should be restricted by privilege/access-control/lifecycle/debug authorization and should not expose fuse data on read muxes when access is denied Observed behavior: Top-level declares fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata and connects them only in the observed excerpt to pkt_wrapper. pkt_wrapper receives reglk_ctrl_i(reglk_ctrl[5]) and acct_ctrl_i(acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5]) while exporting fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o and consuming fuse_rdata_i. The pkt NoC bridge connects external NoC traffic to pkt_axi_req/pkt_axi_resp. Internal pkt_wrapper handling of fuse_rdata, authorization, stale data, and denied-read response is not available. Path: buf_ariane_pkt_noc2_* -> i_pkt_axilite_bridge -> pkt_axi_req -> pkt_wrapper -> fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata -> pkt_axi_resp/NoC response (internal response path not visible) Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Potential exposure of fuse data cannot be confirmed or dismissed; if pkt_wrapper does not mask denied reads, fuse_rdata may be exposed to NoC requesters.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: pkt_wrapper internals, fuse_rdata provider, read-data mux behavior, denied access data masking, and debug/test interactions are not present in provided facts/observations.

findings.summary: PMP pmp_entry NAPOT/NA4 matching appears incorrectly implemented with mask = '1 << size and base = (conf_addr_i << 2) & mask, which checks only one bit rather than a full range mask; this can produce incorrect allow_o decisions despite otherwise sensible priority/default behavior.
findings.vulnerability_category: authorize memory access request / PMP-protected memory regions checked by addr_i and allow_o / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: DMA or core memory access checker using PMP Operation: authorize memory access request Object: PMP-protected memory regions checked by addr_i and allow_o Expected guard: PMP allow decision must correctly enforce configured permissions for matching address ranges, including no-match/default-deny where required and privilege/access-type distinctions Observed behavior: pmp instantiates entries, scans entries in increasing index, applies permissions if priv_lvl is not M or entry is locked, and defaults to allow for M-mode no-match and deny for non-M no-match. However pmp_entry NA4/NAPOT computation sets mask to a single shifted bit and compares (addr_i & mask) == base, rather than masking low address bits for a range. This likely causes false matches/non-matches and incorrect authorization. Path: pmp.addr_i/conf_i/conf_addr_i -> pmp_entry.match_o vector -> pmp priority loop -> allow_o -> requester/DMA PMP use Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: PMP may authorize or deny addresses incorrectly for NA4/NAPOT regions, undermining DMA/memory permission enforcement over protected ranges.

findings.summary: The external debug NoC path reaches dm_master/dm_slave without visible jtag_unlock authorization, while the debug module's own system-bus master response path appears tied off; the NoC debug slave path remains reachable and unmediated by debug authorization.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write memory or peripheral/debug address space via debug bus path / debug module slave/register space and any system memory/peripheral path reachable through dm_master/dm_slave AXI signals / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: debug module/JTAG DMI requester or external debug NoC requester Operation: read/write memory or peripheral/debug address space via debug bus path Object: debug module slave/register space and any system memory/peripheral path reachable through dm_master/dm_slave AXI signals Expected guard: any enabled debug bus-master path must be authorized and range-limited; if intentionally disabled, grants/valid/read-data tie-offs must make the path unreachable Observed behavior: AXI_BUS dm_master is connected through axi2mem to dm_slave_req/we/addr/data and through a debug NoC AXI-lite bridge to external buf_ariane_debug_noc2/noc3 ports. Response signals for a separate debug-module system-bus master path (dm_master_gnt/r_valid/r_rdata) are tied to zero, suggesting that path is disabled. But the NoC bridge still drives dm_master.aw/ar/w channels into axi2mem and dm_slave_* with no visible jtag_unlock, acc_ctrl, or range guard. Path: buf_ariane_debug_noc2_* -> i_debug_axilite_bridge -> dm_master AXI aw/ar/w -> i_dm_axi2mem -> dm_slave_req/we/addr/wdata/rdata -> debug module slave/register path; debug module system-bus master response signals dm_master_gnt/r_valid/r_rdata are tied off Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: External NoC debug requesters may access debug slave/register space without JTAG authorization or range limitation; debug bus-master disablement is incomplete as a security argument because another debug path remains active.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
